# Encoded

> Encoded is a secure, end-to-end encrypted one-time secret sharing platform. Secrets are encrypted in the browser, shared via a one-time link, and permanently destroyed after being viewed once. Built with a zero-knowledge architecture.

Encoded lets users share sensitive information — passwords, credentials, private keys, confidential messages — without leaving a trace in email or chat history. The encryption key is embedded in the URL fragment (`#`), which is never transmitted to the server. The server only ever sees encrypted data.

## How It Works

1. **Write** — Enter your secret. It is encrypted client-side using AES-256-GCM before leaving your browser.
2. **Share** — Send the generated one-time link to the recipient.
3. **Burn** — The secret is permanently deleted after a single view. No backups, no recovery.

## Secret Types

- [Text](https://encoded.org/text): Share encrypted text messages (passwords, keys, notes).
- [File](https://encoded.org/file): Share encrypted files up to 100 GB.
- [Redirect](https://encoded.org/redirect): Create a one-time secret redirect URL.
- [Snap](https://encoded.org/snap): Share self-destructing image snapshots.
- [Neogram](https://encoded.org/neogram): Send a self-destructing animated message, similar to a telegram.

All secret types support optional password protection and configurable expiration (10 minutes to 30 days).

## Security & Trust

- **End-to-End Encryption**: AES-256-GCM via the Web Crypto API. Encryption and decryption happen entirely in the browser.
- **Zero Knowledge**: The encryption key is part of the URL fragment (`#`). Browsers never send fragments to the server. The server cannot decrypt your secrets.
- **Automatic Deletion**: Secrets are permanently removed after a single view. No backups exist.
- **Optional Password Protection**: Adds a second encryption layer. Even with full infrastructure access, the secret cannot be decrypted without the password.
- **Open Source**: Full codebase available under MIT license at [github.com/davelevine/scrt-link-v2](https://github.com/davelevine/scrt-link-v2).
- **Self-Hostable**: Run your own instance — no reliance on a third-party operator.
- **Minimal Dependencies**: Reduced attack surface by design.
- **No Tracking**: No analytics and no third-party cookies. Only essential cookies for authentication.

## API & Client Module

The API supports programmatic creation of text-based secrets (Text, Redirect, Neogram). File and Snap secrets are not available via API due to encryption complexity. Requires an API key from your account page.

### Install

```bash
npm install @scrt-link/client
```

### Usage

```typescript
import { scrtLink, SecretType } from '@scrt-link/client';

const client = scrtLink('your-api-key');

const result = await client.createSecret('my secret content', {
  secretType: SecretType.TEXT,   // 'text' | 'redirect' | 'neogram'
  password: 'optional-password', // optional, adds extra encryption layer
  publicNote: 'For John',       // optional, max 150 chars, visible on link page
  expiresIn: 3600000,           // optional, milliseconds, default 7 days
  host: 'custom.domain.com',    // optional, for white-label setups
});
```

### Response

```json
{
  "secretLink": "https://encoded.org/s#gOOei~kEkcYAAX-YJQnGooSXdSJg8MXkzk~2",
  "receiptId": "D0waygL3",
  "expiresIn": 86400000,
  "expiresAt": "2025-04-24T16:15:52.172Z"
}
```

### ESM Import (no bundler)

```html
<script type="module">
  import { scrtLink } from 'https://encoded.org/api/v1/client-module';
  const client = scrtLink('your-api-key');
  client.createSecret('secret text').then(console.log);
</script>
```

### SecretType Enum

- `SecretType.TEXT` — Encrypted text message
- `SecretType.REDIRECT` — One-time redirect URL
- `SecretType.NEOGRAM` — Self-destructing animated message

## Links

- [Homepage](https://encoded.org): Main landing page.
- [Security](https://encoded.org/security): Detailed security architecture and encryption design.
- [API Documentation](https://encoded.org/api-documentation): Full API reference and client module docs.
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/davelevine/scrt-link-v2): Open-source codebase (MIT license).
- [NPM Package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@scrt-link/client): Client module for programmatic secret creation.
- [About](https://encoded.org/about): About the project and mission.
- [Privacy Policy](https://encoded.org/privacy)
- [Contact](https://encoded.org/contact)
